name: image.read
summary: Deeply analyze an image file: describe it, transcribe all visible text, interpret diagrams/charts. Optionally answer a question about it.

path.description: Path to the image file to analyze

question.description: Optional question to answer about the image

requires: a multimodal backend (Claude) for full understanding, or OCR (mac Vision / Windows OCR / tesseract) for text

name: image.generate
summary: Generate an image from a text prompt (free, no API key) and save it to a file.

prompt.description: What to draw, e.g. 'a watercolor fox in a snowy forest'

out.description: Output path (must be under the OS temp dir or workspace). Default: temp/helm-image-<ts>.jpg

side_effects: writes a generated image file; to show it, end the reply with `ATTACH: <path>`

name: screencap
summary: Capture a screenshot of the Mac screen to a temp file.

name: gui.click
summary: Click at point coordinates on screen (points, not pixels).

name: gui.move
summary: Move the mouse WITHOUT clicking. Target by absolute x,y, OR a named anchor (top-left, top-right, bottom-left, bottom-right, center, top, bottom, left, right), OR a percentage of the screen (xpct/ypct, 0-100). Anchors/percent auto-resolve the screen size, so you can move to a corner without knowing the resolution.

name: gui.size
summary: Return the screen dimensions {width,height,left,top} of the virtual desktop (all monitors). Use to compute coordinates for gui.click / gui.move.

name: gui.scroll
summary: Scroll the mouse wheel at the current cursor (or at x,y). amount = notches; negative = down, positive = up. Pass horizontal:true for sideways scroll.

name: gui.drag
summary: Press the left button at (x,y), drag to (x2,y2), and release — for selecting text, moving items, or drag-and-drop.

name: gui.hotkey
summary: Press a keyboard shortcut / combo: ctrl+c, ctrl+v, ctrl+a, alt+tab, alt+f4, win+r, ctrl+shift+esc, etc. Modifiers (ctrl/alt/shift/win) are held correctly, including the Windows key.

name: app.open
summary: Launch an app by name (e.g. notepad, chrome), open a file/folder by path, or open a URL in the default browser.
side_effects: launches an app / opens a file or URL

name: window.list
summary: List open windows as {pid, app, title}. Run this to get the exact title before window.focus.

name: window.focus
summary: Bring a window to the front by a title substring (e.g. 'Chrome', 'Discord'). Use window.list first for exact titles.

name: clipboard.get
summary: Read the system clipboard text (what the owner last copied).

name: clipboard.set
summary: Write text to the system clipboard. Pair with gui.hotkey ctrl+v to paste long text reliably (more robust than gui.type).

name: gmail.send
summary: Send an email from the owner's Gmail over SMTP. Requires GMAIL_USER + GMAIL_APP_PASSWORD in the vault.

name: gmail.list
summary: List recent emails from the owner's Gmail (or search subject/from/body with query). Returns uid, from, subject, date. Use gmail.read with a uid for the full message.

name: gmail.read
summary: Read one email's full body by uid (from gmail.list). Returns from/to/subject/date/body.

name: gui.type
summary: Type text into the currently focused element.

name: gui.key
summary: Press a key by macOS key code with optional modifiers.

name: imessage.send
summary: Send an iMessage to a handle (phone number or email).

name: discord.attach
summary: Attach a file to the Discord reply by appending ATTACH: /path to stdout.
side_effects: prints ATTACH: line; Discord bot handles the rest

name: memory.remember
summary: Store a structured fact in memory.db.

name: memory.recall
summary: Query memory.db by keywords; returns ranked facts.

name: scheduler.add
summary: Register a new scheduled job in jobs.db.

cron.description: 5-field cron: min hour dom month dow

notify.description: DM owner with the job result when it finishes. Set false for silent housekeeping jobs.

name: scheduler.list
summary: List all scheduled jobs.

name: browser.open
summary: Open a URL in headless Chromium and return page text. Logins persist via workspace/browser-profile/.
requires: Playwright Chromium — auto-installed on first use via npx playwright install chromium
side_effects: saves current URL to workspace/browser-state.json; may write cookies to browser-profile/

name: browser.read
summary: Read text content of the current browser page (set by the last browser.open or browser.click).

name: browser.click
summary: Click a CSS selector on the current page; follows navigation if it occurs.

name: browser.fill
summary: Fill a form input on the current page with text.
side_effects: modifies form field on loaded page

name: browser.screenshot
summary: Screenshot the current browser page viewport.

name: browser.close
summary: Clear the current browser session state (forgets current URL; cookies in profile persist).

name: browser.images
summary: Navigate to a URL in Chromium (reusing any saved login), scroll to load lazy images, and download the page's images — ranked biggest-first — to a folder. No site API needed.

url.description: Page to pull images from, e.g. https://www.facebook.com/<page>/photos

min.description: Skip images smaller than this many bytes (drops tracking pixels / tiny icons)

scroll.description: Scroll passes to trigger lazy / infinite-scroll loading (0-60)

headful.description: Show the browser window while scraping (default: invisible/headless)

requires: Playwright Chromium — auto-installed on first use
For login-walled sites (Facebook, Instagram, X): run browser.login once first
side_effects: writes image files under workspace/downloads/; outbound requests to the page and its image hosts. To show one in chat, end the reply with `ATTACH: <path>`

name: browser.login
summary: Open a URL in a VISIBLE browser window so the owner can sign in once. The session cookies persist in the browser profile, so later browser.open / browser.images calls are already logged in. Use for Facebook, Instagram, X, etc.

seconds.description: How long to keep the window open for sign-in (10-900). Closing the window finishes early.

requires: Playwright Chromium — auto-installed on first use
A visible/unlocked desktop (the owner needs to interact with the window)
side_effects: opens a real browser window; saves login cookies into workspace/browser-profile/ (gitignored, private)

name: imessage.send_to
summary: Send an iMessage to any handle (phone or email). First-class proactive send — not a reply.

handle.description: Phone number (+66...) or Apple ID email

side_effects: sends a real iMessage to the specified handle

name: calendar.list
summary: List upcoming calendar events via Calendar.app (JXA). Falls back gracefully if Calendar is inaccessible.

days.description: How many days ahead to look

name: calendar.add
summary: Add an event to the first writable calendar via Calendar.app.

name: finder.search
summary: Search for files by name or content using mdfind (Spotlight).

name: finder.reveal
summary: Reveal a file or folder in Finder (brings Finder to front).
side_effects: opens Finder, brings window to front

name: web.fetch
summary: Fetch a URL with curl and return stripped plain text. No API key required.

name: web.search
summary: Search the web via DuckDuckGo HTML endpoint. Returns titles, URLs, snippets. No API key.

name: pages.wait
summary: Poll a URL until it actually serves HTTP 200 — so you only tell the owner a site is 'live' once it truly is. GitHub Pages' first build takes ~1 min (the URL 404s until then). Call this AFTER deploying, before saying it's live.

url.description: The site URL to wait for, e.g. https://goodman-pro.github.io/muniqe/

side_effects: none (polls the URL). Returns {live:true,...} once it serves 200, else {live:false,...} on timeout

name: screen.recent
summary: Return last N screen-change events (timestamp, hash, path). Requires screen watcher daemon running.

name: screen.at
summary: Return the screen event closest to a given Unix timestamp (ms).

name: screen.search
summary: Search OCR text from screen events. Only useful when watcher was started with --ocr flag.

name: notify.recent
summary: Return last N notification events (Messages/Calendar/Mail) recorded by the notify poller daemon.

name: notify.unread
summary: Return current unread totals: { messages, calendar, mail }. Queries live; poller not required.
requires: Full Disk Access (Messages), Automation > Calendar, Automation > Mail (all optional; missing returns null)

name: location.here
summary: Return current location { lat, lon, accuracy, ts } via CoreLocationCLI, or { installed: false } if not installed.
requires: CoreLocationCLI (brew install corlocationcli — must ask owner first)

name: mic.record
summary: Record a WAV clip from the default microphone. Uses sox or ffmpeg. Returns path to WAV or install note.
requires: sox (brew install sox) or ffmpeg (brew install ffmpeg) — ask owner before installing

name: mic.transcribe
summary: Transcribe a WAV file using whisper.cpp. Returns { text } or install note if whisper.cpp missing.
requires: whisper.cpp (brew install whisper-cpp — ask owner before installing)

name: vision.describe
summary: Take a screenshot and use Claude to describe what is visible on screen.

out.description: Output path for temp screenshot PNG. Default: /tmp/helm-vision.png

scale.description: Retina display scale factor (pixels÷scale = points). Default: 2

side_effects: writes temp PNG; makes a Claude API call via claude -p

name: vision.find
summary: Take a screenshot and use Claude to locate a UI element; returns its center {x,y} in points for gui.click.

query.description: Description of the UI element to find, e.g. 'Submit button'

out.description: Output path for temp screenshot PNG. Default: /tmp/helm-vision.png

scale.description: Retina display scale factor (pixels÷scale = points). Default: 2

side_effects: writes temp PNG; makes a Claude API call via claude -p

name: vision.verify
summary: Take a screenshot and ask Claude whether an expected screen state is currently visible. Use after gui.click or gui.type to confirm the action worked.

expect.description: Natural-language description of what should be visible, e.g. 'the text field contains hello'

out.description: Output path for temp screenshot PNG. Default: /tmp/helm-vision.png

side_effects: writes temp PNG; makes a Claude API call via claude -p

name: gui.step
summary: Run a shell command, screenshot, verify with Claude that the action succeeded, retry up to N times with failure classification.

cmd.description: Shell command to execute as the GUI action

description.description: Natural-language description of what success looks like on screen

side_effects: executes cmd, writes temp screenshots, makes Claude API calls via claude -p

name: reverse.web
summary: Reverse-engineer a web URL (cross-platform). Leads with a plain-language Summary / What It Does / How It Works, then deep detail: tech stack, live XHR/fetch capture via headless Chromium, AST code analysis, third-party services & SDKs, full API operation catalog with inferred purpose, inferred data model, auth/session mechanics, security posture (CSP/HSTS/etc. explained), forms/data collection, clone scaffold + OpenAPI stub. ALWAYS writes a PDF (and a .md) to workspace/reverse/<slug>-report.pdf.

url.description: URL to analyze (must be a target you own or are authorized to inspect)

name.description: Optional slug for the report filename (default: derived from URL)

requires: Playwright Chromium — auto-installed on first use (used for live network capture + PDF rendering)
side_effects: writes workspace/reverse/<slug>-report.pdf and .md; outbound HTTP request to target. To show it, end the reply with `ATTACH: <pdf path>`

name: reverse.app
summary: Reverse-engineer an app/binary (cross-platform). Leads with a plain-language Summary / What It Does / How It Works, then deep internals. macOS .app: Info.plist, otool -L, entitlements (explained in plain language), plus a deep dive — Electron app.asar package.json/dependencies/main-entry/native-addons, embedded frameworks + versions, CPU architectures, code signing, helper apps, auto-update feed, declared capabilities. Windows PE/Linux ELF: header parse, imports/needed libs, runtime inference. ALWAYS writes a PDF (and .md) to workspace/reverse/<slug>-report.pdf.

path.description: Absolute path to a .app bundle, .exe/.dll, or binary (must be a target you own or are authorized to inspect)

name.description: Optional slug for the report filename (default: derived from basename)

requires: Playwright Chromium — auto-installed on first use (for PDF rendering)
macOS-only deep inspection (otool/plutil/codesign) needs macOS; off-macOS falls back to generic binary analysis

name: reverse.file
summary: Reverse-engineer any file (cross-platform, pure-JS): identify format from magic bytes, hexdump first 256 bytes, extract printable strings. ALWAYS writes a PDF (and .md) to workspace/reverse/<slug>-report.pdf.

path.description: Absolute path to the file to analyze (must be a file you own or are authorized to inspect)

name.description: Optional slug for the report filename (default: derived from basename)

requires: Playwright Chromium — auto-installed on first use (for PDF rendering)

name: mind
summary: Helm Mind — AI-first second brain over HelmBrain. Verbs: save, capture, find, synthesize, research, daily, recap, health. Reads the vault first and writes AI-first notes back (the vault rewrites itself).

input.description: Topic/query/conversation for the verb. Optional for daily/health/recap.

requires: HelmBrain vault present; Claude engine configured
side_effects: reads and updates notes in the HelmBrain vault

name: uia.tree
summary: Walk the UI Automation element tree of the foreground (or titled) window. Returns each control's name, controlType, automationId, and bounding rect + center in physical pixels. Use to discover what is clickable.

title.description: Window title substring; omit for foreground window

name: uia.find
summary: Find a UI control by accessibility name/automationId (substring, case-insensitive). Returns the element and its center {x,y} in physical pixels — feed center straight to gui.click. Far more reliable than vision for real controls.

name.description: Text to match against the control Name or AutomationId

role.description: ControlType filter: Button, Edit, MenuItem, CheckBox, ComboBox, Tab, List, ...

title.description: Window title substring; omit for foreground window

exact.description: Require exact match instead of substring

name: uia.invoke
summary: Find a UI control and activate it via UI Automation patterns (Invoke/Toggle/Select) WITHOUT moving the mouse — the most reliable click. Falls back to returning center coords (invoked:false) if no pattern is supported.

name.description: Text to match against Name or AutomationId

side_effects: activates a UI control (click-equivalent, no mouse movement)

name: ocr.read
summary: Screenshot the screen and OCR it with the built-in Windows engine. Returns all visible text plus per-word/line boxes with absolute pixel coords. For reading text Helm cannot get from the DOM/UIA.

name: ocr.find
summary: Find visible on-screen text by substring and return its center {x,y} for gui.click. Use for canvas apps, games, images, or anything UI Automation cannot reach.

name: window.state
summary: Get the foreground window's title, pid, app, rect {x,y,w,h}, and maximized/minimized state.

name: window.move
summary: Move a window (matched by title substring) so its top-left is at (x,y).

name: window.resize
summary: Resize a window (matched by title substring) to width w, height h, keeping its position.

name: window.snap
summary: Snap/arrange a window on its current monitor: left|right|top|bottom half, max, min, restore, or center.

name: window.close
summary: Gracefully close a window (matched by title substring) via WM_CLOSE — the app may prompt to save. Does NOT force-kill.

name: macro.list
summary: List saved input macros.

name: macro.save
summary: Save an explicit macro: a JSON array of steps ({type:move|click|type|key|hotkey|scroll|drag|wait, ...}).
side_effects: writes a macro file under workspace/macros/

name: macro.record
summary: Record a macro by sampling cursor position + mouse-button clicks for N seconds.
side_effects: writes a macro file; read-only while recording

name: macro.replay
summary: Replay a saved macro, injecting its recorded mouse/keyboard actions.

name: computer.click_element
summary: One-step: find a UI control by name (UI Automation) and click its center. The reliable way to click a named button/menu/field. Returns {clicked, element}.

name: computer.click_text
summary: One-step: find visible on-screen text by substring (OCR) and click its center. For clicking labels/links not exposed to UI Automation.

name: solve
summary: Elite structured problem-solver: decompose -> 2-3 approaches -> critique -> best solution with concrete steps, assumptions, risks, confidence. Engine-backed.

mode.description: quick (1 LLM call, default) or deep (4 calls, higher quality)

name: video.generate
summary: Text-to-video: expand a prompt into N scenes, generate AI images, assemble an H.264 mp4 with Ken Burns motion + crossfades; optional voice narration.

name: flow.list
summary: List saved automation flows.

name: flow.show
summary: Show a saved flow's steps.

name: flow.save
summary: Save an automation flow: an array of steps (tool/args or exec, with saveAs vars + when conditions).

json-file.description: path to a JSON file of steps

name: flow.run
summary: Run a saved flow: interpolate {{vars}}, evaluate when-conditions, chain tool/shell steps.

name: flow.delete
summary: Delete a saved flow.

name: files.find
summary: Recursively find files by name glob, content substring, age (days), or min size. Skips node_modules/.git.

name: files.tree
summary: Compact directory tree.

name: files.big
summary: List the N largest files under a directory.

name: files.dupes
summary: Find byte-identical duplicate files (sha256) + wasted bytes.

name: files.organize
summary: Plan/apply moving files into subfolders by type or YYYY-MM date. Dry-run unless apply=true.
side_effects: moves files when apply=true (dry-run by default)

name: files.rename
summary: Bulk rename via regex match + replacement ($1..). Dry-run unless apply=true.
side_effects: renames files when apply=true (dry-run by default)

name: files.zip
summary: Compress a file/folder into a .zip (Compress-Archive).

name: files.unzip
summary: Extract a .zip (Expand-Archive). Dry-run lists contents unless apply=true.

name: doc.info
summary: PDF metadata: page count, title, author.

name: doc.extract
summary: Extract text from a PDF.

name: doc.merge
summary: Merge multiple PDFs into one.

name: doc.split
summary: Split a PDF into one file per page.

name: doc.topdf
summary: Render txt/markdown/html to a PDF (Chromium).

name: data.summary
summary: Profile a CSV/JSON: rows + per-column type & stats.

name: data.query
summary: Filter/select/sort/limit rows from CSV/JSON (ops = != > >= < <= contains).

name: data.agg
summary: Group-by aggregation (sum/avg/count/min/max).

name: data.chart
summary: Render a bar/line/pie chart PNG from CSV/JSON via QuickChart (free, no key).

name: audio.voices
summary: List installed Windows SAPI TTS voices.

name: audio.say
summary: Text-to-speech to a WAV via Windows SAPI.

name: audio.info
summary: Audio metadata via ffprobe.

name: audio.convert
summary: Convert audio between formats (ffmpeg).

name: audio.trim
summary: Trim an audio clip (ffmpeg).

name: audio.transcribe
summary: Transcribe audio via whisper if installed.

name: research
summary: Deep multi-source web research with inline citations; saves a markdown report.
side_effects: web requests + claude; writes a report

name: image.info
summary: Image dimensions + format.

name: image.resize
summary: Resize an image (optionally keep aspect).

name: image.crop
summary: Crop an image region.

name: image.rotate
summary: Rotate an image 90/180/270.

name: image.flip
summary: Flip an image horizontally/vertically.

name: image.convert
summary: Convert image format (by out extension).

name: image.grayscale
summary: Convert an image to grayscale.

name: image.annotate
summary: Draw text onto an image.

name: system.stats
summary: System snapshot: CPU%, memory, disks, uptime, OS, hostname.

name: system.top
summary: Top processes by cpu or mem.

name: system.ps
summary: List processes (optionally filter by name).

name: system.net
summary: Active TCP connections.

name: system.disk
summary: Drive free/total.

name: system.kill
summary: Terminate a process by pid or name (refuses system-critical).

name: translate
summary: Translate text or a file into any language via the claude engine.

name: screen.record.start
summary: Start a screen recording to mp4 (ffmpeg gdigrab). Bounded if seconds given, else detached.
side_effects: writes an mp4; may leave a detached recorder

name: screen.record.stop
summary: Stop the active detached screen recording.

name: screen.record.status
summary: Is a screen recording active?

name: screen.record.gif
summary: Convert an mp4 to an optimized GIF (ffmpeg).
